# Troubleshooting {#sec-troubleshooting}

This chapter collects the issues most likely to interrupt a session, grouped
by where they show up.

## Install & launch

**PowerShell blocks the venv activation script.**
Allow signed local scripts for your user, then activate again:

```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser
```

**`ModuleNotFoundError` when launching the app.**
`PYTHONPATH` must point at `src`. Use
`$env:PYTHONPATH = "src"` (PowerShell) or `PYTHONPATH=src` (macOS/Linux/WSL)
before `python -m frontend.main`.

**Tests can't run / `pytest` not found.**
You installed `requirements.txt` (runtime-only) instead of
`requirements-dev.txt`. Reinstall with `pip install -r requirements-dev.txt`.

## Phase 1 (offline)

**Load Data fails.**
A BrainVision recording needs all three files together — `.vhdr`, `.vmrk`,
and `.eeg` — in the same folder. Point the picker at the folder containing
them.

**The bad-channel or ICA window didn't appear.**
Both are **modal** MNE windows that open during preprocessing. They can open
behind the main window — check the taskbar. Preprocessing waits until you
close them.

**No artifact after Train.**
`decoder_pipeline.joblib` is written to the output directory chosen in
**Settings** (step 1), not the working directory. Confirm Train reported
"Trained at: N ms" and check that directory.

## Phase 2 (live)

**No stream appears during discovery.**
- Confirm the stream is actually publishing (`NeuroneStream`, type `EEG`).
- Click **Refresh** to scan again.
- Remember the live path is **Windows-only** (`LSLProxy.exe`).

**Inference stuck on "Connecting…".**
The inlet could not be established. Re-check the stream is up (@sec-hardware),
then **Halt** and **Start** again.

**Probabilities look wrong or flat.**
- Confirm the **sampling rate** matches what the decoder was trained for —
  this is a known item to verify in the lab (1000 Hz vs 5000 Hz).
- Confirm you loaded the correct `decoder_pipeline.joblib` and selected the
  intended **target**.

**Triggers not detected.**
Triggers are embedded in the EEG stream's trigger channel (index 64), not a
separate marker stream, and are decoded as `(raw >> 8) & 0xFF`. Verify the
parallel-port codes match `markers_mapping` in your config (@sec-configuration).

## Getting more detail

The app and backend log according to the conventions in
[`docs/architecture/logging.md`](../../architecture/logging.md). Raising the
log level there gives more visibility into discovery, the inference loop, and
session shutdown.
